Let $n$ be a positive integer and let $x_1, \dots, x_n$ be $n$ nonzero points
in $\mathbb{R}^2$. Suppose $\langle x_i, x_j \rangle$ (scalar or dot product)
is a rational number for all $i, j$ ($1 \le i, j \le n$). Let $S$ denote all
points of $\mathbb{R}^2$ of the form $\sum_{i=1}^n a_i x_i$ where the $a_i$ are
integers. A closed disk of radius $R$ and center $P$ is the set of points at
distance at most $R$ from $P$ (includes the points distance $R$ from $P$).
Prove that there exists a positive number $R$ and closed disks $D_1, D_2,
\dots$ of radius $R$ such that

\begin{enumerate}[(a)]
\item Each disk contains exactly two points of $S$;
\item Every point of $S$ lies in at least one disk;
\item Two distinct disks intersect in at most one point.
\end{enumerate}
